reason: The remediation only edits a PAM password quality setting (lcredit) in /etc/security/pwquality.conf, a standard STIG hardening step. It does not affect bootloader, kernel, or filesystem layout and is unlikely to cause lockout if the value is set to a reasonable negative number (e.g., -1).
estimated_impact: authentication/authorization affected; Potential for password policy to be too strict, causing user password changes to fail; Misconfiguration could temporarily prevent password changes until corrected
